questions.question: Write a C function to insert a node at the beginning of a doubly linked list.
keyPoints: Set head->prev to newNode (if head exists)

questions.question: Explain why deleting a node (when given its pointer) is O(1) in doubly linked list but O(n) in singly linked list.
keyPoints: Singly: Need to find previous node by traversing from head
Doubly: Have direct access to prev pointer
Can update prev->next and next->prev directly
No traversal needed in doubly linked list

questions.question: Write a function to reverse a doubly linked list.
keyPoints: Swap prev and next pointers for each node
Move to next node using prev (since swapped)
Swap head and tail at the end

questions.question: Compare singly and doubly linked lists in terms of memory usage and operation complexities.
keyPoints: Memory: Doubly uses extra pointer per node
Delete from end: Singly O(n), Doubly O(1) with tail
Delete given node: Singly O(n), Doubly O(1)
Insert before node: Singly O(n), Doubly O(1)
Traversal: Singly forward only, Doubly both directions
